business_ideas.idea: Digital meet and greets: pay to video chat with celebrity for 1 min via Zoom, scheduled queue system
Cameo variations: personalized shout-out videos from celebrities, but cheaper or different formats
Winter.io: copywriting testing platform finding right demographic to test your messaging
UCalc.pro: no-code embedded calculator tool for websites to boost SEO and engagement
Portfolio of niche WordPress plugins: OptinMonster, WPForms, Monster Insights, WP Mail SMTP
User testing marketplace: people submit photos then receive videos of 250 reactions unboxing product
Deep Senegal: home security monitoring with live human guard response service
Printing network via Airbnb model: neighbors with printers earn $5 per job instead of FedEx
Browser extension for user testing feedback: micropay testers $3 per quick video review while browsing

predictions.text: Diversity quotas in personal social media recommendation lists are counterproductive to authentic sharing
Twitter will become place where women tech creators can build massive audiences, currently male-dominated

disagreements: Sam vs Shaan on diversity in Twitter follows: Sam says personal taste shouldn't require quotas, Shaan says as their platform grows they should model inclusivity. Both agreed quotas are wrong but disagreed whether influence creates responsibility. Shaan: 'we have crossed threshold of influence.' Sam countered: 'if I have voice I should get what interests me.'
dynamics: Sam drives idea generation from his research and network; Shaan provides critique and deeper dives. Energy is enthusiastic and idea-focused. Sam shares from personal experiments (user testing mom, Deep Senegal at Noah Kagan's). Dynamic shows Sam finding and Shaan validating or pushing back.
frameworks: Marketplace dynamics: supply side first (testers/celebrities) then demand side
Bottom-up virality: products that spread through company because one person finds them useful
Bottoms-up adoption where anyone at company can sign up for tool, not top-down enterprise sales
learnings: Boring task-removal businesses (transcription, testing, calculations) are high-margin if they remove enough friction
Newsletter businesses work when they combine free audience funnel with high-end paid tier for specialists
WordPress plugin portfolio model works because creators build huge audience then promote adjacent tools
Instagram harassment and diversity criticism became viral attention mechanic that amplified original post reach
Personal anecdotes (mom as user tester) validate market more than hypothetical examples

banger_quotes.quote: Most people in Silicon Valley are like, oh what a shame, I can't say back
The nature of the product is that you share the results, that spreads through company
If I'm not thinking about diversity when tweeting, maybe that's the problem
Playing business: when you get domain, cards, logo, and swag before getting any customers

numbers_dropped: User Testing at $100M+ recurring revenue from 2000 organizations
UCalc creator is Russian, appears bootstrapped, no visibility online
Saeed Balkhi's plugins estimated $30-40M recurring revenue range
WPBeginner gets 5-10M monthly uniques, probably makes $10M+ per year
Winter testing platform raised money but was less than year old
Sam gained 20,000 followers from single Twitter thread about favorite accounts
notable: Sam got roasted on Twitter for all-male list of favorite accounts, sparked conversation about diversity in tech Twitter. Instagram diversity conversation showed power of relatable memes to go viral.
